title: MFFC — the "private cone" of a node

steps.title: Setup: target = t, cut = {p, q, e, h} (two internal ANDs + two PIs)
steps.narrative:
An MFFC w.r.t. a cut is the set of internal ANDs that would die if t were replaced — but only counting nodes above the cut. Cut leaves and everything below them must stay alive (the replacement may reuse them).

The interesting case is when cut leaves are internal ANDs (like p, q here): without protection, dereferencing would walk through them into their fanins and absorb structure that doesn't belong in the MFFC. The boundary boost is what stops that.

Every node shows its fanouts count next to it as [N]. Watch them update.

panes.title: Cut: { p, q, e, h }
panes.body: The cone of t bounded by this cut is { t, m, n }. Anything below the cut (a, b, c, d, p's and q's fanin cones) is treated as "already exists" and must not be absorbed.

steps.title: Counterfactual: what would happen WITHOUT the boost
steps.narrative:
Imagine we skipped the boundary boost. Abc_NodeRefDeref(t, deref) would walk:

deref(t) → deref(m) → deref(p) → ... a, b are PIs (return 0). count for p added.
then deref(q) → c, d. count for q added.
then deref(n) → e, h.

Result: count = 5; MFFC = {t, m, n, p, q} — wrongly absorbing p and q. Any replacement built on top of {p, q, e, h} would lose its references to p, q after commit.

entries.Problem: p and q absorbed into MFFC — they're cut leaves and should survive

steps.title: Apply boundary boost: +1 on every cut leaf
steps.narrative: abcRewrite.c:138..148 brackets the deref with a +1/-1 on each cut-leaf's fanout count. The boost guarantees no leaf's count can reach 0 during deref — recursion stops at the cut.

steps.title: Deref t → count = 1
steps.narrative: deref(t): count = 1, mark t (assign current TravId). t's fanouts go 1→0 too, but that's the root itself — counted in the running total directly.

steps.title: Deref m (t.fanin0): 1 → 0 → recurse, count = 2
steps.narrative: Decrement m.fanouts: 1 → 0 → recurse into m, mark it.

steps.title: Boost saves the day: deref p (m.fanin0): 2 → 1, NOT zero — STOP
steps.narrative: m's fanin0 is p, a cut leaf. Boosted count: 2. Decrement → 1. Not zero → recursion STOPS. p is not added to the MFFC. Without the boost, p would have gone 1 → 0 here and we would have walked past the cut.

steps.title: Same for q (m.fanin1): 2 → 1, STOP
steps.narrative: q is the other cut leaf reached from m. Same outcome: 2 → 1, no recurse, q not in MFFC.

steps.title: Deref n (t.fanin1): 1 → 0 → recurse, count = 3
steps.narrative: Back at t, deref the second fanin n. n only feeds t → its count was 1. Decrement → 0 → recurse, mark n. count = 3.

steps.title: n's fanins (e, h) are PI cut leaves — boost holds
steps.narrative: e and h are PI cut leaves. Their boosted counts decrement (2 → 1) but stay positive. Recursion stops, count finalises at 3.

steps.title: Result: MFFC = {t, m, n}, nNodesSaved = 3
steps.narrative:
Recursion done. MFFC has the 3 red nodes — exactly the cone above the cut. p, q, e, h survive (blue) thanks to the boost; everything below them (a, b, c, d) is untouched.

Without the boost, count would have been 5 and p, q would have been wrongly tagged. The +1/-1 trick is the difference between a correct MFFC and a wrong one.

entries.Untouched: a, b, c, d (and p, q's fanin cones)

panes.title: Re-reference pass
panes.body: Abc_NodeMffcLabelAig follows up with a re-ref pass walking the same red path in reverse, restoring every fanout count. Then the +1 boost on cut leaves is stripped. The AIG is byte-identical to its original state — only the red nodes carry the current TravId.
